audit_items.proof_trace: GetPixelWriteMask() returns a Quantum scalar (unsigned char/short/int or float depending on build config), not a pointer — NULL-checking its return value is logically inapplicable. Every caller across the codebase uses the return value in a numeric comparison (e.g. `<= (QuantumRange/2)`), never as a pointer dereference. The function always returns a valid scalar: either QuantumRange (when the channel trait is undefined) or a pixel channel offset value.
